_source: BYTE-GROUNDED catalog decoded 2026-07-10 from the ORIGINAL US PSX disc EXE (SLUS_012.79, name/desc glyph strings via catalog table RAM 0x80019770) cross-checked against the PC port table Dino2.exe VA 0x704260 (same 12-byte records, same flags/iconUV, JP glyph strings at ptr+0/ptr+4). Authored RE knowledge (offsets + names as identifiers), no game bytes. Supersedes the earlier guide-sourced placeholder file. See docs/decisions/dc2/ITEMS-KEYITEMS-RE-PLAN.md and K75.
_encoding_note: US SLUS names are word-per-glyph strings: glyph/2 -> 1..26=A..Z, 27..52=a..z, 0=space, 0x6a+2n=digit n, 0x82='?', 0x90='-', 0x9a='.', 0x9c=',', 0x9e=apostrophe; 0xf000=line break, 0x4000=end. PC port carries the same table with JAPANESE katakana/kanji glyph strings (0x7244c4 region).
_id_spaces: TWO DISTINCT id spaces. (1) THIS catalog id = the INVENTORY item id (record scene_mgr+0x10bc + slot*12, byte+1; equipped main/sub at scene_mgr+0x10b4/+0x10b5, sub stored as id|0x10; ITEM.DAT icon block index == this id, loader 0x403ed0). (2) The op-0x31 room-script byte (data/dc2/item-placements.json, ids 1..9) is the AOT SAT-1 VM-THREAD SLOT, param = routine index -- NOT this catalog id (see K77). The field-type -> catalog-id give mapping is the open decode.

catalog.name: Shockwave
catalog.note: name string prefixed by two blank lines in the source table

catalog.name: Machete Improved
catalog.note: native transform target: EXE 0x40ceaf rewrites inventory id 0x11 -> 0x17

catalog.name: Large Stungun (improved)
catalog.note: native transform target: EXE 0x40cf57 rewrites inventory id 0x12 -> 0x18; US name string identical to 0x12

catalog.name: Key plate
catalog.note: ids 0x25-0x2a are six Key plate rows sharing one name/desc string (US ptr 0x8008c528)

catalog.name: Signal Bullet
catalog.note: key-item duplicate of sub 0x19 (same display name, capital B)

_owner_flags_note: flags word (rec+0xA, PSX-truth per data/dc2/weapon-catalog.json): 0x01 Regina / 0x02 Dylan / 0x04 David owner bits + 0x08 MAIN / 0x10 SUB class + 0x20 wide-icon. Weapon rows carry the weapon-catalog.json values; item rows (0x1a+) are flags 0x0000.

_field_pickup_types._source: CE live capture 2026-06-27 + decode_script op-0x31 census (165 items / 54 rooms, data/dc2/item-placements.json). This is the SEPARATE op-0x31 id space (SAT-1 VM-thread slot, K77) whose item correlation is empirical, id histogram 1=95,2=31,3=14,4=6,5=8,6=5,7=3,8=2,9=1.

types.1: Med Pak S (nominal) -- but the GRANTED catalog id is NOT fixed: live 2026-07-12, a type-1 ST102 pickup on Normal (byte[scene+0x108E]=1) granted catalog 0x1c (Med Pak M), NOT 0x1b. type != catalog; health ids are difficulty-remapped at give time (K98).
types.2: Resusc. Pak (nominal; catalog 0x1e -- but see type-1 note: verify per difficulty)
types.5: Dino File (live-witnessed; dino-file log, NOT an inventory catalog row)
types.7: ammo pickup (live qty 7; ammo merges into the weapon record +8, no catalog row)

_field_pickup_types._give_path: RESOLVED (LIVE, K98, 2026-07-12). The granted catalog id = word[itemRec+2] set by the pickup routine[param] (K77), NOT the op-0x31 id byte (byte=1 != granted 0x1c). Give core 0x40CE70: SetFlag(9, word[itemRec+2], 1) @0x40CE79 = ownership (bank 9 = 0x878098). Consumables (health 0x1a-0x1f) ALSO get a bag count record via 0x49F900 (bag = scene+0x11AC; find-in-bag 0x496010). Key items (0x21-0x34) = ownership flag ONLY, no record (live: City Keycard = catalog 0x22 -> flag(9,0x22), both banks byte-identical). Dino files (type 5) = flag/log only. Lever for a shuffler = the routine's granted catalog id (word[itemRec+2]); health is difficulty-scaled at give time.
_field_pickup_types._mapping_open: The type->catalog mapping is per-routine (and difficulty-dependent for health), not a fixed table; the op-0x31 id byte is a VM-thread slot (K77), NOT the item lever.

dinoFiles._source: guide-sourced (FAQ); dino files route to the log, not inventory -- no catalog rows
